text:
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
THORACIC / ONCOLOGIC PATHOLOGY DIVISION

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PATIENT: Margaret Tran
MRN: [REDACTED]
DOB: [REDACTED]
SEX: Female
ORDERING PROVIDER: [Thoracic Oncology Attending]
CLINICAL SERVICE: Medical Oncology / Thoracic Oncology
SPECIMEN RECEIVED: [DATE]
SPECIMEN REPORTED: [DATE + 3 days]
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SPECIMEN ID: SP-[REDACTED]-16

CLINICAL HISTORY:
Patient is a 62-year-old female with previously diagnosed stage IIIB (T3N2M0) right lower lobe lung adenocarcinoma, EGFR L858R-mutant (diagnosed approximately 14 months prior), treated with Tagrisso (Tagrisso) 80 mg daily as first-line therapy. Presented for restaging evaluation following a period of excellent partial response; interval restaging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis revealed enlargement of the primary right lower lobe mass from 1.8 cm to 3.1 cm, recurrent ipsilateral mediastinal lymphadenopathy, new bilateral pulmonary nodules, and a new 1.4 cm right adrenal nodule not present on prior imaging. Concurrent brain MRI demonstrated three new intracranial enhancing lesions. CT-guided core needle biopsy of the right adrenal nodule performed to confirm suspected metastatic disease and to obtain adequate tissue for molecular resistance mechanism profiling.

PROCEDURE TYPE:
CT-guided percutaneous core needle biopsy, right adrenal gland
Guidance: CT fluoroscopy
Approach: Posterior retroperitoneal
Needle gauge: 18-gauge coaxial system, 20-gauge core biopsy needle
Number of core passes: 4
Procedure performed by: Interventional Radiology
Clinical indication: New adrenal nodule on restaging imaging; evaluation for metastatic lung adenocarcinoma vs. primary adrenal pathology; tissue procurement for molecular resistance testing

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS PATHOLOGY:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Received in formalin are four core needle biopsy fragments labeled "right adrenal nodule." Cores are tan-white to tan-pink, firm, and measure in aggregate 1.4 x 0.2 x 0.2 cm (combined). Individual cores range from 0.3 to 0.5 cm in length. Two cores are submitted entirely for histologic processing in one cassette (A1); one core entirely for ancillary studies/FFPE block for molecular testing (A2); one core processed for touch preparation cytology and then submitted (A3). Material is adequate for diagnosis.

Representative sections: All material submitted.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSCOPIC FINDINGS:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Histologic sections reveal cores of adrenocortical and fibroadipose tissue infiltrated by a malignant epithelial neoplasm arranged in acinar (glandular) and solid growth patterns. The neoplastic cells are moderately pleomorphic, polygonal to columnar, with moderate amounts of pale eosinophilic to amphophilic cytoplasm, and vesicular nuclei with prominent nucleoli. Mitotic figures are readily identified (approximately 6/10 high-power fields). Areas of central necrosis are present within larger glandular clusters. Stromal desmoplasia is noted at the periphery of the tumor deposit. No definitive lepidic growth is appreciated in this adrenal biopsy material, consistent with a metastatic rather than primary pulmonary origin. The background adrenocortical parenchyma shows mild cortical cell vacuolization consistent with compression atrophy; no primary adrenocortical neoplasm is identified.

The morphologic features, including glandular architecture, nuclear grade, and clinical context, are consistent with metastatic adenocarcinoma.

Touch imprint cytology (A3): Cellular preparation showing loosely cohesive clusters and single malignant epithelial cells with enlarged nuclei, prominent nucleoli, and moderate cytoplasm. Cytomorphology consistent with adenocarcinoma.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (IHC):
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
A panel of immunostains was performed on FFPE sections from block A1 and evaluated in comparison with appropriate positive and negative controls.

TTF-1 (clone SP141): POSITIVE -- Strong diffuse nuclear staining in greater than 80% of tumor cells. This finding supports pulmonary origin.

Napsin A: POSITIVE -- Granular cytoplasmic staining in approximately 60% of tumor cells, further supporting lung adenocarcinoma primary.

CK7 (clone OV-TL 12/30): POSITIVE -- Diffuse, strong cytoplasmic staining in tumor cells.

CK20: NEGATIVE -- No staining in tumor cells. Excludes colorectal and other CK20-positive primaries.

p40 (deltaNp63): NEGATIVE -- No nuclear staining in tumor cells. Excludes squamous cell carcinoma.

Synaptophysin: NEGATIVE

Chromogranin A: NEGATIVE -- The above two markers effectively exclude a neuroendocrine (including pheochromocytoma) etiology.

Melan-A (MART-1): NEGATIVE
Inhibin-alpha: NEGATIVE -- Excludes adrenocortical carcinoma.

Calretinin: NEGATIVE

PD-L1 (22C3 pharmDx, Dako): POSITIVE -- TPS 20-25% (upgraded from 15% on prior biopsy; minor intratumoral heterogeneity may account for this difference; clinically not significantly changed). CPS not formally interpretable on core biopsy material.

Note: PD-L1 result is informational. Interpretation in clinical context required.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
MOLECULAR / ANCILLARY STUDIES:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded (FFPE) tissue from block A2 submitted for comprehensive next-generation sequencing (NGS) solid tumor panel (523-gene panel, tumor-normal paired analysis) including evaluation for EGFR resistance mechanisms (T790M, C797S, exon 20 insertions), copy number alterations (MET, ERBB2, CDK4), fusion analysis, and full somatic mutation profiling. Results reported separately (see molecular pathology report).

EGFR mutational status by PCR (reflex rapid testing, cobas EGFR Mutation Test v2): EGFR L858R (exon 21) DETECTED. T790M (exon 20) status: DETECTED (preliminary, confirmation by NGS panel pending). Note: Rapid EGFR PCR result is informational and intended to expedite clinical decision-making; comprehensive NGS results supersede.

FISH for MET amplification: PENDING (to be resulted separately).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PATHOLOGIC STAGING (pTNM, AJCC 8th Edition):
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
This biopsy confirms metastatic disease. In the context of the patient's known right lower lobe primary lung adenocarcinoma:

pM1c: Distant metastasis to adrenal gland confirmed (extrathoracic visceral metastasis)

Overall pathologic stage: Stage IV (pTxNxM1c) -- in conjunction with known prior staging.

Note: pT and pN cannot be assigned from this biopsy specimen alone.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SPECIMEN ADEQUACY:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Specimen is adequate for diagnosis and for ancillary molecular testing. Tumor cellularity in block A2 (submitted for NGS) estimated at 40-50%, sufficient for molecular profiling per laboratory criteria (minimum 20% required).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DIAGNOSIS:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
RIGHT ADRENAL GLAND, CT-GUIDED CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY:

METASTATIC ADENOCARCINOMA, CONSISTENT WITH LUNG PRIMARY
-- Morphology: acinar and solid growth pattern, moderately differentiated
-- IHC profile: TTF-1+, Napsin A+, CK7+, CK20-, p40-, synaptophysin-, chromogranin-, inhibin-, Melan-A-
-- PD-L1 TPS approximately 20-25% (22C3 assay)
-- EGFR L858R detected by reflex PCR; T790M detected (preliminary)
-- Comprehensive NGS resistance profiling pending (see separate molecular report)
-- No evidence of primary adrenal neoplasm (adrenocortical carcinoma, pheochromocytoma excluded)

Correlate with clinical and radiographic findings.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ATTENDING PATHOLOGIST COMMENT:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
The histologic and immunophenotypic features of this right adrenal core biopsy are diagnostic of metastatic adenocarcinoma of pulmonary origin. The tumor cells demonstrate strong TTF-1 and Napsin A positivity with CK7 expression, a profile highly specific for lung adenocarcinoma primary in this clinical context. Primary adrenal malignancies (adrenocortical carcinoma, pheochromocytoma) are effectively excluded by the absence of inhibin, Melan-A, synaptophysin, and chromogranin staining. The morphology (acinar-predominant glandular architecture with moderate nuclear pleomorphism, focal necrosis, and desmoplastic stroma) is consistent with metastatic lung adenocarcinoma and differs from the lepidic-acinar pattern described in the original right lower lobe biopsy, a finding in keeping with phenotypic evolution at a metastatic site under prior TKI selective pressure.

The detection of EGFR L858R by reflex PCR confirms clonal relationship to the known primary. Preliminary T790M detection is consistent with acquired Tagrisso resistance; comprehensive NGS will delineate the full resistance landscape including assessment for bypass mechanisms.

This case was reviewed at sign-out by the attending thoracic pathologist.

Signed: [Attending Pathologist, MD], Thoracic Pathology
Reviewed by: [Senior Resident / Fellow, MD]

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ELECTRONICALLY SIGNED AND VERIFIED
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
